title: `(trunc nuw A) == (trunc nuw B)` should not take multiple `xor`s [InstCombine]
summary:
The original issue is a very specific missed fold: an `icmp` on values that were both reduced in width first, especially the boolean case
`icmp eq (trunc nuw X to i1), (trunc nuw Y to i1)`.
Semantically, the compare only depends on low bits/known-range information from the original operands, but InstCombine failed to recognize it early enough, so the obvious simplification was missed.

The patch turns that one pattern into a general `icmp`-of-`trunc`/`ext` optimization by invoking `foldICmpTruncWithTruncOrExt()` earlier in `visitICmpInst`, before other canonicalization/known-bits folds interfere. The abstraction is: if an `icmp` compares operands formed by width-changing casts (`trunc` vs `trunc`, or `trunc` vs `zext`/`sext`) and the cast flags (`nuw`/`nsw`) guarantee the discarded or added bits are irrelevant, rewrite the compare in terms of the original values or a simpler equivalent compare. Key generalization points are: support non-equality predicates, mixed cast kinds, and only require one side to carry the needed no-wrap fact when sufficient.

The dev tests expand coverage beyond the motivating `i1 eq` case:
- `trunc_unsigned_nuw_zext`: mixed `trunc` + `zext`, unsigned relational predicate (`ult`);
- `trunc_equality_bool`: preserves the original boolean-equality case;
- `trunc_equality_either`: equality between two truncs where either side may provide the decisive wrap guarantee (`nuw` on one side, `nsw` on the other depending on legality of the fold).

This is a good template: identify a missed one-off cast/compare fold, then lift it to a cast-shape + predicate + nowrap-property matcher and add tests across predicate kinds and operand asymmetry.
